sections.heading: RT
sections.content: {"role": "RT is used in multiple settings: as definitive therapy for unresectable disease, as adjuvant therapy after surgery (especially for incomplete resection or higher-stage disease), and as local therapy for recurrent or metastatic lesions [90].", "principles": ["Recommendations should be made by radiation oncologists with experience in managing thymomas and thymic carcinomas [THYM-B].", "Definitive RT for unresectable disease, incompletely resected invasive thymoma or thymic carcinoma, or as adjuvant therapy after systemic therapy and surgery [THYM-B].", "Close communication between radiation oncologist and surgeon regarding operative findings is essential to define the target volume at risk [THYM-B].", "Communication with pathologist regarding histology, disease extent, and surgical margins is important [THYM-B].", "Review of preoperative imaging and co-registration into the planning system are helpful [THYM-B].", "Extensive elective nodal irradiation (ENI) is not recommended because thymomas do not commonly metastasize to regional lymph nodes [7, THYM-B]."], "dose_frameworks": [{"name": "Adjuvant RT, clear/close margins", "total_dose": "45–50 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2.0 Gy", "fractions": "25–28", "schedule": "Conventional fractionation", "indication": "Postoperative adjuvant treatment for clear or close surgical margins [THYM-B]."}, {"name": "Adjuvant RT, microscopically positive margins (R1)", "total_dose": "54 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2.0 Gy", "fractions": "30", "schedule": "Conventional fractionation", "indication": "Postoperative adjuvant treatment for microscopically positive (R1) resection margins [THYM-B]."}, {"name": "Definitive RT for unresectable disease or gross residual disease (R2)", "total_dose": "60–66 Gy", "dose_per_fraction": "1.8–2.0 Gy", "fractions": "33–37", "schedule": "Conventional fractionation", "indication": "For patients with unresectable disease or gross residual disease (R2) after surgery [3, THYM-B]."}, {"name": "Palliative RT", "total_dose": "Various (e.g., 8 Gy in 1 fx, 20 Gy in 5 fx, 30 Gy in 10 fx up to definitive doses)", "dose_per_fraction": "Varies", "fractions": "Varies", "schedule": "Based on treatment objectives", "indication": "Palliative setting; given the long natural history, even definitive doses may be considered for durable local control [THYM-B]."}], "approaches": [{"name": "3D Conformal Radiation Therapy (3D-CRT)", "dose_fractionation": "As per dose frameworks", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "As per clinical setting (e.g., cisplatin/etoposide, carboplatin/paclitaxel)", "indication": "Minimum technological standard for RT [THYM-B].", "key_trial": "Not specified.", "toxicities": "Standard RT toxicities; conservative heart dose limits recommended [THYM-B]."}, {"name": "Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy (IMRT) / Volumetric Modulated Arc Therapy (VMAT)", "dose_fractionation": "As per dose frameworks", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "As per clinical setting", "indication": "Preferred over 3D-CRT to improve target coverage and dose conformity and further reduce cardiac dose [THYM-B].", "key_trial": "Not specified.", "toxicities": "May reduce cardiac dose compared to 3D-CRT."}, {"name": "Proton Therapy", "dose_fractionation": "As per dose frameworks", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "As per clinical setting", "indication": "Appropriate and shown to improve dosimetry, allowing for better sparing of normal organs (lungs, heart) with favorable local control and toxicity [8,9, THYM-B].", "key_trial": "Not specified.", "toxicities": "Favorable normal organ sparing (lungs, heart) [8,9]."}, {"name": "Stereotactic Ablative Radiotherapy (SABR/SBRT)", "dose_fractionation": "High dose per fraction (e.g., 50 Gy in 5 fractions)", "concurrent_chemotherapy": "Not typically concurrent", "indication": "May be appropriate for limited focal metastases [114,115, THYM-B].", "key_trial": "Not specified.", "toxicities": "High local control rates with acceptable toxicity for small metastases."}]}

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT, clear/close margins
timeline.body: Total dose: 45–50 Gy, Fractions: 25–28, Dose per fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy, Schedule: Conventional fractionation, Indication: Postoperative adjuvant treatment for clear or close surgical margins [THYM-B].

timeline.title: Adjuvant RT, microscopically positive margins (R1)
timeline.body: Total dose: 54 Gy, Fractions: 30, Dose per fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy, Schedule: Conventional fractionation, Indication: Postoperative adjuvant treatment for microscopically positive (R1) resection margins [THYM-B].

timeline.title: Definitive RT for unresectable disease or gross residual disease (R2)
timeline.body: Total dose: 60–66 Gy, Fractions: 33–37, Dose per fraction: 1.8–2.0 Gy, Schedule: Conventional fractionation, Indication: For patients with unresectable disease or gross residual disease (R2) after surgery [3, THYM-B].

timeline.title: Palliative RT
timeline.body: Total dose: Various (e.g., 8 Gy in 1 fx, 20 Gy in 5 fx, 30 Gy in 10 fx up to definitive doses), Fractions: Varies, Dose per fraction: Varies, Schedule: Based on treatment objectives, Indication: Palliative setting; given the long natural history, even definitive doses may be considered for durable local control [THYM-B].
